name: Monolith Shell
description: A vertical ovoid or lozenge, aspect ratio approximately 1:2.8 (taller than wide). Surface is basalt-like stone with subtle iridescent clearcoat at grazing angles. Does not take off — dissolves into mist. Interior is luminous white void. Hovering 28 feet above ground. Denis Villeneuve's Arrival (2016) is the canonical cinematic reference. Anti-saucer minimalism. The monolith class embodies the 'Turrell interior' aesthetic — interior light as revelation.

ir_signature.ir_notes: Cinematic archetype, not based on sensor footage. Would read as cold-body in IR — the basalt surface absorbs rather than emits.

cultural_context: Denis Villeneuve's Arrival (2016) — the Shell design drew on Turrell's light installation work and 2001's monolith. The lack of saucer-ness was intentional: a craft that doesn't reference any human vehicle grammar. Villeneuve and production designer Patrice Vermette called it 'a tool, not a vehicle.' The mist-dissolve departure and the luminous white interior are its defining aesthetic moments.
